maxContractTerm.value: No statutory maximum found. Virginia statute does not set a maximum term that a school division may agree to with a private student-transportation contractor. Title 22.1, Chapter 12 (Pupil Transportation) is silent on contract term. The Virginia Public Procurement Act (VPPA), Va. Code Title 2.2, Ch. 43, governs school-division procurement of services but contains no general cap on the term of a services contract; the only explicit term limits in the VPPA apply to architectural/professional-engineering term contracts (Va. Code § 2.2-4303.1: one year, renewable for three additional terms) and job order contracting (Va. Code § 2.2-4303.2: one year, renewable for three additional one-year terms), neither of which reaches pupil-transportation services.
maxContractTerm.citation: Va. Code §§ 22.1-176 through 22.1-187 (Pupil Transportation, Article 1) — no provision on contract term (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacodefull/title22.1/chapter12/article1/); Va. Code § 2.2-4303 (Methods of procurement) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4303/); Va. Code § 2.2-4303.1 and § 2.2-4303.2 (term limits expressly confined to A/E term contracting and job order contracting, respectively) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4303.1/ and https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4303.2/); Va. Code § 2.2-4309 (Modification of the contract — no general term cap) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4309/).

rebidRequired.label: Required
rebidRequired.rule: At the expiration of an awarded term, a Virginia school division must re-procure pupil-transportation services through a competitive process (competitive sealed bidding or competitive negotiation) under Va. Code § 2.2-4303(A): 'All public contracts with nongovernmental contractors for the purchase or lease of goods, or for the purchase of services, insurance, or construction, shall be awarded after competitive sealed bidding, or competitive negotiation as provided in this section, unless otherwise authorized by law.' A school division qualifies as a 'public body' subject to the VPPA, and Va. Code § 2.2-4343(A)(11) confirms applicability by permitting a school board to be excused from particular VPPA articles only if it has adopted its own competition-based alternative policies. Renewal of an existing contract beyond its original term without re-competition is permitted only where the original solicitation reserved priced renewal options (so the renewal was part of the competed award) or where another statutory exemption applies (sole source, emergency, cooperative procurement). Mere extension under Va. Code § 2.2-4309(B) is limited to 'completion of any work undertaken but not completed during the original term.'
rebidRequired.citation: Va. Code § 2.2-4303(A) (competitive sealed bidding/competitive negotiation required) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4303/); Va. Code § 2.2-4301 (definition of 'public body') (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4301/); Va. Code § 2.2-4343(A)(11)–(A)(12) (school-division alternative-policy exemption based on competitive principles; continued application of specified VPPA provisions) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4343/); Va. Code § 2.2-4309(B) (extension only to complete unfinished work) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4309/).

renewalMechanics.value: No statutory renewal mechanism specific to student-transportation contracts. Under the VPPA, a school division may include priced renewal options in the original solicitation (so the renewal is part of the originally competed and awarded contract) and exercise them by mutual agreement or unilateral option as drafted, subject to the contract's own terms. The VPPA also expressly allows extension under Va. Code § 2.2-4309(B) only 'to allow completion of any work undertaken but not completed during the original term of the contract' — not a substantive renewal. Cooperative/'piggyback' procurement is independently authorized under Va. Code § 2.2-4304 (Joint and cooperative procurement). The VPPA does not impose CPI/index caps, fuel-escalator rules, board-approval thresholds, evergreen prohibitions, or maximum-renewal-count rules on student-transportation contracts; those mechanics, if any, are set by individual school-board policy and the solicitation itself, not by state statute. The two VPPA term-contracting renewal frameworks that do exist (Va. Code § 2.2-4303.1: three additional renewal terms; Va. Code § 2.2-4303.2: three additional one-year terms) apply only to architectural/engineering term contracts and job order contracting, respectively, and do not govern pupil transportation.
renewalMechanics.citation: Va. Code § 2.2-4309(B) (extension to complete unfinished work) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4309/); Va. Code § 2.2-4304 (joint and cooperative procurement) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacodefull/title2.2/chapter43/article2/); Va. Code § 2.2-4303.1 and § 2.2-4303.2 (renewal limits applicable only to A/E term contracting and job order contracting) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4303.1/ and https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4303.2/); Va. Code Title 22.1, Ch. 12 (silent on renewal of pupil-transportation contracts) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacodefull/title22.1/chapter12/).

finalFields.statutoryCitation: Primary: Virginia Public Procurement Act, Va. Code Title 2.2, Ch. 43 (§§ 2.2-4300 to 2.2-4377) — in particular Va. Code § 2.2-4301 (Definitions, including 'public body') (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4301/), Va. Code § 2.2-4303 (Methods of procurement — competitive sealed bidding and competitive negotiation) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4303/), Va. Code § 2.2-4304 (Joint and cooperative procurement) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacodefull/title2.2/chapter43/article2/), Va. Code § 2.2-4309 (Modification of the contract; extension only to complete unfinished work) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4309/), Va. Code § 2.2-4343(A)(11)–(A)(12) (school-division alternative-policies exemption, conditioned on competition-based principles; continued mandatory compliance with specified VPPA sections) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter43/section2.2-4343/). Transportation-specific authority: Va. Code Title 22.1, Ch. 12 (Pupil Transportation) — Va. Code § 22.1-176 (school board authority to provide transportation) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title22.1/chapter12/section22.1-176/), Va. Code § 22.1-177 (Board of Education school-bus regulations), and 8VAC20-70 (Regulations Governing Pupil Transportation) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/admincodefull/title8/agency20/chapter70/) — none of which set a contract-term limit, rebid requirement, or renewal mechanic for student-transportation procurement. School board contracting authority generally: Va. Code § 22.1-79(3) and (8) (powers and duties; public-hearing requirement before transferring noninstructional services to a private entity) (https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title22.1/chapter7/section22.1-79/).

districtVariance.label: Yes
districtVariance.explanation: Because Virginia statute is silent on the maximum term and renewal mechanics for pupil-transportation contracts and Va. Code § 2.2-4343(A)(11) expressly permits each school board to adopt 'alternative policies and procedures' that are 'based on competitive principles and generally applicable,' material variance across school divisions is structurally permitted and observed in practice. Different school divisions adopt different initial terms and renewal-option structures within their own procurement policies and individual solicitations (e.g., Virginia Beach City Public Schools' published RFPs use a five-year initial term with up to five additional one-year renewal options by mutual agreement). The statewide rule that does not vary is the underlying VPPA requirement of competitive sealed bidding or competitive negotiation under Va. Code § 2.2-4303.

specialEducation.detail: No separate Virginia statute or Board of Education regulation sets different term limits, rebid requirements, procurement rules, or renewal mechanics for special-education transportation, IEP-mandated transportation, McKinney-Vento transportation, foster-care transportation, or vendors serving students with disabilities. Va. Code Title 22.1, Ch. 12 (Pupil Transportation) and 8VAC20-70 do not differentiate procurement rules by student population, and the VPPA does not contain a special-education carve-out for contracting procedures. Federal IDEA and McKinney-Vento obligations affect the duty to provide transportation but, on Virginia-law research as of the access date, do not change the state contract-term or procurement rules. Not located: any Virginia AG opinion or VDOE guidance setting a distinct procurement track for special-education transportation contracting.

perFieldVerdicts.note: Title 22.1 Ch. 12 and 8VAC20-70 re-opened; both silent on contract term. VPPA Article 2 (Va. Code §§ 2.2-4303 through 2.2-4324) re-opened section-by-section; the only term-limit provisions are § 2.2-4303.1 (A/E term contracts) and § 2.2-4303.2 (job order contracts), neither of which reaches pupil transportation. § 2.2-4309 re-opened and contains no general term cap. No disconfirming authority surfaced. 'No statutory maximum found' is the correct negative result.

Va. Code § 2.2-4303(A) re-opened and verified verbatim: 'All public contracts with nongovernmental contractors for the purchase or lease of goods, or for the purchase of services, insurance, or construction, shall be awarded after competitive sealed bidding, or competitive negotiation as provided in this section, unless otherwise authorized by law.' § 2.2-4301 'public body' definition and § 2.2-4343(A)(11)–(A)(12) school-division exemption framework verified — alternative policies must remain 'based on competitive principles.' § 2.2-4309(B) confirmed to permit only extension for completion of unfinished work, not substantive renewal.

No statutory renewal mechanism specific to pupil transportation. § 2.2-4309(B) extension language verified verbatim; cooperative procurement authority under § 2.2-4304 verified. § 2.2-4303.1 and § 2.2-4303.2 confirmed as limited to A/E and JOC, not transportation. No CPI/escalator/board-approval/evergreen statutory constraints found on transportation contracts.
All cited official URLs re-opened successfully on law.lis.virginia.gov (the official LIS publisher) and Virginia Administrative Code. Each cited section's text matches the rule it is offered for.

Statutory silence on transportation contract term + § 2.2-4343(A)(11) authority for divisions to adopt their own competition-based policies make divisional variance the statutorily-permitted state. Published divisional RFP/practice examples confirm variance in initial terms and renewal-option structures. The constant statewide rule is the underlying competitive-procurement requirement of § 2.2-4303.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max contract term — 'No statutory maximum found'
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A general or transportation-specific cap exists in Title 22.1 Ch. 12, in VPPA Article 2, in § 2.2-4309, or in 8VAC20-70 (e.g., 'not exceed', 'multi-year', 'successive', 'annual termination').
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened Va. Code Title 22.1 Ch. 12 section list and § 22.1-176; re-opened § 2.2-4303, § 2.2-4303.1, § 2.2-4303.2, § 2.2-4309; reviewed 8VAC20-70 section list. WebSearch for 'Virginia code school division pupil transportation contract term limit statute' and 'Virginia VPPA section 2.2-4309 school division services contract maximum term'.
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority. The only VPPA term-limit provisions are § 2.2-4303.1 (A/E) and § 2.2-4303.2 (JOC), neither of which reaches pupil transportation. Title 22.1 Ch. 12 and 8VAC20-70 contain no term-limit provision. A 'high-risk contract' definition referenced in some sources is a procurement-oversight designation (anticipated term > 5 years), not a cap.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: An exemption permits non-competitive renewal at term-end (sole source, emergency, cooperative purchasing, piggyback, school-board waiver, or alternative-policies exemption that drops competition).
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened § 2.2-4303 (methods of procurement and explicit exception language), § 2.2-4304 (joint/cooperative procurement), § 2.2-4309 (extension only to complete unfinished work), and § 2.2-4343(A)(11)–(A)(12) (school-division alternative-policies exemption, which still requires policies 'based on competitive principles').
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority. Exemptions exist (cooperative purchasing under § 2.2-4304; sole source / emergency under § 2.2-4303(F)/(G) and small-purchase thresholds under § 2.2-4303(G)–(H)) but they are exceptions to competitive procurement methodology, not blanket permission to skip competition for a routine pupil-transportation re-award. The school-division alternative-policies exemption (§ 2.2-4343(A)(11)) expressly requires competition-based principles.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal mechanics — no statutory CPI/escalator/board-approval/evergreen rules on pupil-transportation contracts
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A VPPA provision or 8VAC20-70 rule constrains renewal terms (CPI cap, fuel-escalator standard, max number of renewals, public-hearing requirement, board approval threshold) specifically for transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened § 2.2-4309 (the only modification rule, and even it caps fixed-price increases >25% / $50,000 absent governing-body approval); searched § 2.2-4303.1 and § 2.2-4303.2 to confirm their renewal frameworks are confined to A/E and JOC; reviewed 8VAC20-70 section list for any renewal-mechanics provision.
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority. The 25%/$50,000 cap in § 2.2-4309(A) governs fixed-price modifications, not renewal of pupil-transportation contracts. § 22.1-79(8) imposes a public-hearing requirement before initial transfer of noninstructional services to a private entity, not on every renewal. Renewal mechanics for transportation are creatures of the solicitation and division policy, not state statute.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A separate VPPA or Title 22.1 carve-out or 8VAC20-70 provision sets distinct term/rebid/renewal rules for IEP/McKinney-Vento/foster-care/special-education transportation contracting.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Reviewed Title 22.1 Ch. 12 section list; reviewed VPPA exemption article (§ 2.2-4343 and neighbors); WebSearch for 'Virginia school division special education transportation contract procurement separate rules'.
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority. Va. Code Title 22.1, 8VAC20-70, and the VPPA contain no separate procurement carve-out for special-education transportation contracting. Federal IDEA and McKinney-Vento create duties to transport but do not alter Virginia contract-term or procurement rules.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Uniform statewide guidance (VDOE bulletin, AG opinion, or School Boards Association manual) imposes a single term/renewal standard across divisions, eliminating practical variance.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searched VDOE pupil-transportation guidance pages; searched for VSBA model policy on pupil-transportation contract term; reviewed § 2.2-4343(A)(11)–(A)(12) (which permits and structures divisional variance through alternative policies); reviewed published divisional examples (Virginia Beach 5+5 RFP structure).
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority. § 2.2-4343(A)(11) structurally permits divisional variance, and divisional RFP/practice examples confirm different term/renewal structures across divisions. The constant statewide rule is the competitive-procurement requirement of § 2.2-4303.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Recent session-law amendments to § 2.2-4303, § 2.2-4309, § 2.2-4343, or Title 22.1 Ch. 12 changed the contract-term, rebid, or renewal analysis after the official code-currency date.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Reviewed law.lis.virginia.gov consolidated section pages (which integrate enacted amendments); reviewed VPPA 'unofficial copy' published by DGS for July 1, 2024 update; searched LIS bill database for 2024–2025 session amendments to the cited sections.
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority. The substantive rules in § 2.2-4303 (competitive procurement requirement) and § 2.2-4343(A)(11)–(A)(12) (school-division alternative-policies framework) are long-standing; § 2.2-4303.2 was updated effective July 1, 2024 to set the annual JOC cap at $10 million, but that change does not reach pupil transportation. No amendments materially change the contract-term, rebid, or renewal analysis for pupil-transportation contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: Va. Code Title 22.1, Ch. 12 (Pupil Transportation), §§ 22.1-176 to 22.1-198 (Articles 1 and 2)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Code currency: as published on the Virginia Legislative Information System (law.lis.virginia.gov), the official LIS publisher; effective text in force as accessed.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Fields 1, 2, 3 (negative — chapter is silent on contract term, rebid, and renewal mechanics for private pupil-transportation contracts)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Chapter 12 addresses school board authority to provide pupil transportation (§ 22.1-176), nonpublic-school transportation agreements (§ 22.1-176.1), waivers (§ 22.1-176.2), Board of Education school-bus regulations (§ 22.1-177), driver requirements, insurance, and similar safety-and-operations matters; no section sets a contract term, mandates a rebid cycle, or establishes a renewal mechanic for student-transportation procurement.

sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current Code of Virginia as published on the Virginia Legislative Information System.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Fields 1, 2, 3 (background — confirms transportation is discretionary except as required by § 22.1-221; no contract-term provisions)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'School boards may provide for the transportation of pupils, but nothing herein contained shall be construed as requiring such transportation except as provided in § 22.1-221.' Silent on private-contractor terms.

sourceLedger.citation: Va. Code § 2.2-4301 (Definitions — Virginia Public Procurement Act)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current Code of Virginia as published on the Virginia Legislative Information System.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Fields 2, 4 (definition of 'public body' establishing VPPA applicability to school boards/political subdivisions)
sourceLedger.relevantText: '"Public body" means any legislative, executive or judicial body, agency, office, department, authority, post, commission, committee, institution, board or political subdivision created by law to exercise some sovereign power or to perform some governmental duty, and empowered by law to undertake the activities described in this chapter.' Re-verified — school boards qualify as 'board' / political subdivision under this definition; § 2.2-4343(A)(11)'s express reference to school divisions confirms applicability.

Re-verified verbatim: 'All public contracts with nongovernmental contractors for the purchase or lease of goods, or for the purchase of services, insurance, or construction, shall be awarded after competitive sealed bidding, or competitive negotiation as provided in this section, unless otherwise authorized by law.' Establishes that re-award at term-end requires a competitive process unless a statutory exemption applies. Section also permits exceptions via written determinations for impracticability/fiscal-disadvantage, and small-purchase thresholds ($80,000–$300,000 depending on category).

sourceLedger.citation: Va. Code § 2.2-4303.1 (Architectural and professional engineering term contracting; limitations)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 1 (negative — to show this explicit term-limit provision does NOT reach pupil transportation)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Limits term to 'one year or when the cumulative total project fees reach the maximum authorized in this section, whichever occurs first,' with three additional renewal terms; expressly applies to architectural and professional engineering services only. Re-verified.

sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 1 (negative — confirms VPPA term limits are confined to JOC, not student transportation)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Limits job order contract to a one-year term, renewable for three additional one-year terms at public-body option; $10M annual cap effective July 1, 2024; individual job limit of $1M per job order. Highway/transportation work generally prohibited from JOC. Re-verified.

sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 3 (cooperative/piggyback procurement as a permissible alternative to competitive rebid)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Authorizes joint and cooperative procurement by public bodies (the statutory basis for school-division use of cooperative or 'piggyback' contracts in lieu of running an independent procurement).

sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Fields 1, 3 (limits on contract modification and extension; no general term cap)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Re-verified. Subsection (A): 'no fixed-price contract may be increased by more than twenty-five percent of the amount of the contract or $50,000, whichever is greater, without the advance written approval of the Governor or his designee' for state agencies or the governing body for political subdivisions. Subsection (B): 'Any public body may extend the term of an existing contract for services to allow completion of any work undertaken but not completed during the original term of the contract.' No subsection sets a maximum initial term.

sourceLedger.citation: Va. Code § 2.2-4343(A)(11) and (A)(12) (Exemption — school divisions adopting alternative competition-based policies; mandatory continued compliance)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Fields 2, 5 (confirms VPPA applies to school divisions; permits but constrains district variance through alternative policies)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Re-verified. (A)(11) exempts a school division whose school board has adopted alternative policies and procedures that are '(i) based on competitive principles and (ii) generally applicable to procurement of goods and services by the school board,' provided such policies remain in effect. (A)(12) requires continued compliance, notwithstanding subdivisions 9–11, with subsections B, C, and D of § 2.2-4303 and enumerated VPPA sections (§§ 2.2-4305, 2.2-4311, 2.2-4315, 2.2-4317, 2.2-4330, 2.2-4333 through 2.2-4338, 2.2-4342, 2.2-4343.1, 2.2-4367 through 2.2-4377) and Chapter 43.1; and continues professional-services competitive-negotiation requirements where cost exceeds $80,000.

sourceLedger.citation: Va. Code § 22.1-79 (Powers and duties of school boards)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 4 (school-board contracting authority and public-hearing requirement for transferring noninstructional services to private entities)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Re-verified. Subdivision (3): authority to 'care for, manage, and control the property of the school division and provide for the erecting, furnishing, equipping, and noninstructional operating' of property. Subdivision (8): public-hearing requirement (with at least seven days' newspaper notice) before 'the transfer from the public school system of the administration of all instructional services for any public school classroom or all noninstructional services in the school division pursuant to a contract with any private entity or organization.'

sourceLedger.citation: 8VAC20-70 (Regulations Governing Pupil Transportation), promulgated by the Virginia Board of Education
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Virginia Administrative Code as published; chapter governs pupil-transportation operations.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Fields 1, 2, 3 (negative — addresses bus specifications, driver requirements, safety, and operations, not contract term/rebid/renewal mechanics)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Re-verified. Chapter is organized in Parts I–V (Definitions; General Regulations; School Bus Driver Requirements; General Requirements for School Buses; School Activity Buses). None of the parts contain a contract-term limit, rebid requirement, or renewal mechanic for student-transportation contracts.

sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 4 (definition acknowledging private-contractor operation of school buses for public boards)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Recognizes vehicles 'owned or operated by any person under contract by, a county, city, town, or school board' transporting pupils — confirming private contracting is recognized; no contract-term provisions.

sourceLedger.citation: Virginia Beach City Public Schools, RFP (school transportation-related solicitation) — published example of district practice
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Published 2022-2023; district practice example, not state law.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 5 (district variance — illustrates district-set term/renewal structure used under VPPA discretion)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The initial contract term will be for five (5) years, with options to renew annually for up to five (5) additional years, upon agreement of both parties.' Used as evidence of district-level practice/variance, not as authority for any statewide rule.

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Stage 2 re-opened every official URL in the Stage 1 pack and confirmed text. Disconfirming searches were run against (a) Title 22.1 Ch. 12 section-by-section for any term/renewal provision; (b) VPPA Article 2 (§§ 2.2-4303 through 2.2-4324) for any general services-contract term cap; (c) § 2.2-4309 for any subsection setting a maximum initial term; (d) 8VAC20-70 section list for any contract-mechanics provision; (e) Va. Code § 22.1-79 for the public-hearing requirement; (f) WebSearch for any disconfirming statewide guidance on transportation contract term/renewal; (g) WebSearch for any special-education transportation procurement carve-out; (h) divisional RFPs (e.g., Virginia Beach CPS, FCPS, LCPS) as district-practice/variance evidence only — not as authority for statewide rules.
verificationNotes.currentLawCheck: Code of Virginia accessed via Virginia Legislative Information System (law.lis.virginia.gov), the official publisher; the site presents the consolidated current Code as updated through enacted legislation. Specific 'current through' date not visibly stamped on each section page; recorded as the consolidated Code as accessed on 2026-06-24. Virginia Administrative Code (8VAC20-70) similarly accessed on the official site as currently effective. Cross-checked § 2.2-4303.2 reflecting July 1, 2024 JOC annual-cap update at $10M (does not affect pupil-transportation analysis).
verificationNotes.unresolvedIssues: None. All five fields resolved. No authoritative counter-evidence surfaced.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No conflict between Title 22.1 Ch. 12 / 8VAC20-70 and the VPPA — the transportation-specific authority is silent on contract mechanics and the VPPA supplies the operative competitive-procurement rule. No conflict between general VPPA exemption framework and school-division application — § 2.2-4343(A)(11)–(A)(12) integrate by expressly requiring competition-based principles and continued compliance with enumerated VPPA sections.

notesSpecialProvisions: Practical-application notes for Virginia, all anchored to official authority: (i) Small-purchase thresholds — Va. Code § 2.2-4303(G)–(H) permits small-purchase procedures below specified thresholds ($80,000–$300,000 depending on category) provided written procedures are adopted; competition must still be pursued 'wherever practicable.' (ii) Sole-source and emergency procurement — Va. Code § 2.2-4303(F)–(G) permit non-competitive procurement upon written determination of sole-source justification or emergency, with such competition 'as is practicable under the circumstances.' (iii) Cooperative purchasing / piggyback — Va. Code § 2.2-4304 expressly authorizes joint and cooperative procurement; school divisions routinely satisfy procurement obligations by piggybacking eligible cooperative contracts in lieu of an independent solicitation. (iv) School-division alternative policies — Va. Code § 2.2-4343(A)(11) permits a school board to adopt 'alternative policies and procedures' exempting it from particular VPPA articles only if such policies are 'based on competitive principles and generally applicable'; § 2.2-4343(A)(12) requires continued compliance with enumerated VPPA sections (including subsections B, C, and D of § 2.2-4303, professional-services competitive negotiation when cost exceeds $80,000, and §§ 2.2-4305, 2.2-4311, 2.2-4315, 2.2-4317, 2.2-4330, 2.2-4333 through 2.2-4338, 2.2-4342, 2.2-4343.1, 2.2-4367 through 2.2-4377). (v) Public hearing before transferring noninstructional services — Va. Code § 22.1-79(8) requires a public hearing (with at least seven days' newspaper notice) before a school board transfers administration of all noninstructional services in the division to a private contractor; this requirement attaches to the initial transfer decision and is not waivable by solicitation drafting. (vi) Contract-modification cap — Va. Code § 2.2-4309(A) caps fixed-price modifications at 25% or $50,000 (whichever is greater) absent governing-body approval, providing a practical ceiling on mid-term price increases. (vii) Charter schools — Virginia public charter schools operate under the authority of and within local school divisions (Va. Code §§ 22.1-212.5 et seq.), so the same VPPA rules and school-board policies apply to their transportation procurement. (viii) Special-education transportation — no separate Virginia statute or 8VAC20-70 rule sets distinct procurement procedures; federal IDEA and McKinney-Vento obligations affect the duty to transport but do not displace the state procurement framework. (ix) State-approval requirement — there is no statewide state-agency pre-approval mandate for school-division pupil-transportation contract awards; VDOE establishes minimum school-bus specifications and oversees DGS competitive bid processes for the statewide bus-purchase contract, but routine division-level service-contract awards are not subject to VDOE pre-approval.
exceptionsUnresolved: All five fields resolved for Virginia. No fields left unresolved after Stage 2 adversarial review. The negative result on Field 1 ('No statutory maximum found') is itself a documented finding: Title 22.1 Ch. 12 section-by-section, VPPA Article 2 section-by-section, § 2.2-4309 subsection-by-subsection, and 8VAC20-70 Parts I–V were all re-opened, and the only explicit term limits located (§ 2.2-4303.1 and § 2.2-4303.2) expressly apply only to A/E term contracting and job order contracting, respectively. Special-education differentiation remained 'No' after targeted search for separate IDEA/McKinney-Vento/foster-care/disability transportation procurement rules; none located in Virginia statute, regulation, or VDOE guidance.
